result.stdout:
USAGE: mig [--help] [--version] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    init <options>        initialize a schema-matched database from a compiled
                          generated module
    codegen <options>     generate Db.fs and query helpers from Schema.fs plus
                          compiled schema types
    migrate <options>     create new database and copy data from old using a
                          compiled generated module
    offline <options>     run a one-shot offline migration using a compiled
                          generated module
    plan <options>        show a dry-run migration plan using a compiled
                          generated module
    drain <options>       stop writes on old database and replay accumulated
                          changes
    cutover <options>     mark new database as ready for serving
    archive-old <options> archive the old database after cutover
    reset <options>       reset failed migration artifacts on old/new databases
    status <options>      show current migration state

    Use 'mig <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --version, -v         print mig version
    --help                display this list of options.

commands.payload:
USAGE: mig [--help] [--version] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    init <options>        initialize a schema-matched database from a compiled
                          generated module
    codegen <options>     generate Db.fs and query helpers from Schema.fs plus
                          compiled schema types
    migrate <options>     create new database and copy data from old using a
                          compiled generated module
    offline <options>     run a one-shot offline migration using a compiled
                          generated module
    plan <options>        show a dry-run migration plan using a compiled
                          generated module
    drain <options>       stop writes on old database and replay accumulated
                          changes
    cutover <options>     mark new database as ready for serving
    archive-old <options> archive the old database after cutover
    reset <options>       reset failed migration artifacts on old/new databases
    status <options>      show current migration state

    Use 'mig <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --version, -v         print mig version
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
USAGE: mig codegen [--help] [--dir <path>] [--assembly <path>]
                   [--schema-module <name>] [--module <name>] [--output <path>]

OPTIONS:

    --dir, -d <path>      directory that contains Schema.fs and generated
                          source files (default: current directory)
    --assembly, -a <path> compiled assembly that contains schema types for
                          generated Db.fs code
    --schema-module, -s <name>
                          compiled schema module name when using --assembly
                          (default: Schema)
    --module, -m <name>   module name for generated F# code (default: Db)
    --output, -o <path>   output file name in the schema directory (default:
                          Db.fs)
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
USAGE: mig cutover [--help] [--dir <path>] [--assembly <path>] [--module <name>]

OPTIONS:

    --dir, -d <path>      directory that contains Schema.fs and
                          <dir>-<hash>.sqlite files (default: current directory)
    --assembly, -a <path> compiled assembly that contains a generated Db-style
                          module
    --module, -m <name>   compiled module name when using --assembly (default:
                          Db)
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
USAGE: mig init [--help] [--dir <path>] [--assembly <path>] [--module <name>]

OPTIONS:

    --dir, -d <path>      directory that contains the target sqlite location
                          for the generated Db module (default: current
                          directory)
    --assembly, -a <path> compiled assembly that contains a generated Db-style
                          module
    --module, -m <name>   compiled module name when using --assembly (default:
                          Db)
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
USAGE: mig reset [--help] [--dir <path>] [--assembly <path>] [--module <name>]
                 [--dry-run]

OPTIONS:

    --dir, -d <path>      directory that contains Schema.fs and
                          <dir>-<hash>.sqlite files (default: current directory)
    --assembly, -a <path> compiled assembly that contains a generated Db-style
                          module
    --module, -m <name>   compiled module name when using --assembly (default:
                          Db)
    --dry-run             print reset impact without dropping old migration
                          tables or deleting the new database
    --help                display this list of options.
